headline: Malaysia Data Sovereignty Law Requires Local Storage by 2027
context: Parliament passed Data Sovereignty Act requiring all Malaysian citizen data stored within national borders by January 2027.

cards.big: Data Sovereignty Act requires all citizen data stored locally by 2027
cards.sub: Law affects 28 major platforms and 450 enterprise cloud services

cards.big: Compliance requires RM4.5 billion in local data centre investment
cards.sub: International tech companies warned of service disruption during transition

cards.big: Local data centre capacity must grow 45% to absorb required storage
cards.sub: Current 85 centres would need expansion to approximately 120 by deadline

cards.big: Critics argue localization increases costs without improving security
cards.sub: PDPA reform would better protect data than geographic storage requirements

cards.big: Data sovereignty tests whether geographic control of data provides real citizen protection
Data sovereignty with mandatory local storage by 2027. Localisation costs must be weighed against security benefits.
